resolutions.action: Added a new Methodology section describing the literature review process, including databases searched, date range, search keywords, inclusion and exclusion criteria, and how the three benefits were selected from the broader literature.

Replaced generic platitudes with three evidence-grounded, role-specific recommendations including coverage thresholds derived from the cited literature, specific tooling categories, team workflow guidance, and measurable success criteria.

Restructured Section 3 from a three-bullet list into three dedicated subsections, each containing a precise definition, the mechanism by which the benefit is produced, empirical evidence with attribution, and conditions under which the benefit is realized or diminished.

Expanded the Introduction's reference to the ROI debate from an unresolved aside into a brief, cited characterization of both sides, with an explicit statement of how this report's scope is positioned relative to the debate.

Replaced the single Beck (2002) citation and undated, unspecific Stack Overflow reference with eight specific citations including peer-reviewed studies, an IEEE reference, a dated Stack Overflow survey with a named statistic, and Microsoft and IBM empirical findings. Each citation is tied to a specific finding or claim.

Expanded the Limitations section to address both the testing-practice limitations and the report's own scope limitations, including context-dependence of evidence, recency constraints, and domains excluded from the findings.
Replaced the self-referential phrase 'This report summarizes each briefly' with a sentence conveying the significance of the findings, including a concrete data point and the primary recommendation.

Acknowledged that the report was submitted unchanged across three cycles. All prior feedback from cycles 1 and 2 is addressed in this revision. A revision notes section has been added to document the response to each category of prior feedback.
